Vanillyl butyl ether, an ether of monohydroxybenzoic acid, is added to food products as a flavoring agent. It is also present in cosmetics and personal care products as a fragrance ingredient, oral care agent, hair conditioning agent, and warming or cooling agent.
Local antiprotozoal and antifungal agent that may also be given orally.

Flavone, a class of flavonoids, mainly found in spices and red or purple plant foods with antioxidant, anti-proliferative, anti-tumor, anti-microbial, estrogenic, acetyl cholinesterase, anti-inflammatory activities and are also used in cancer, cardiovascular disease, neurodegenerative disorders etc.

Histamine, an organic nitrogenous compound, is involved in local immune responses regulating physiological function in the gut and acting as a neurotransmitter for the brain, spinal cord, and uterus. It is a potent H1 and H2 receptor agonist.
Veratric acid, a simple benzoic acid derived from plants and fruits, has anti-oxidant, anti-inflammation, and blood pressure-lowering effects.
Vindoline is a chemical precursor to vinblastine and exhibits antimitotic activity by inhibiting microtubule assembly.
Fusidine, isolated from Fusidium coccineum, is a steroidal antibiotic that inhibits protein synthesis.
Lobeline hydrochloride is an alkaloid that acts on nicotinic cholinergic receptors but is less potent than nicotine. It is also a VMAT2 ligand and an antagonist of μ-opioid receptors.
Catechins are a plant derived polyphenolic anti-oxidants with phytotoxic properties. (+)-Catechin hydrate is a frequent component of traditional herbal remedies, such as Uncaria rhynchophylla.
Protocatechuic acid (PCA), a dihydroxybenzoic acid, is a type of widely distributed naturally occurring phenolic acid.
(-)-Borneol, one of enantiomers of borneol, has a camphoraceous and evident woody odor.

Zinc Undecylenate is a natural or synthetic fungistatic fatty acid, antifungal Zinc Undecylenate is used topically in creams against fungal infections, eczemas, ringworm, and other cutaneous conditions.
Pyridoxine, also known as vitamin B6, is a form of vitamin B6 found commonly in food and used as dietary supplement. It is a cofactor for both glutamic acid decarboxylase and GABA transaminase.
Batyl alcohol, isolated from shark liver oil, is also present in yellow bone marrow of animals and has been described as an inflammatory agent.

Caryophyllene oxide, an oxygenated terpenoid existed in many plant essential oil, is well known as preservative in food, drugs and cosmetics with anti-inflammatory, anti-carcinogenic and skin penetration enhancing  properties.

Nordihydroguaiaretic acid (NDGA) is a phenolic antioxidant found in the leaves and twigs of the evergreen desert shrub, Larrea tridentata (Sesse and Moc. ex DC) Coville (creosote bush). It is a recognized inhibitor of lipoxygenase (LOX) and has antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties.
Methyl 4-hydroxybenzoate is an anti-fungal agent often used in a variety of cosmetics, personal-care products and food preservatives.
Synonyms: Methylparaben, Methyl paraben, Methyl p-hydroxybenzoate, Nipagin, Methyl parahydroxybenzoate

Information: L-Arabinose (l-Ara) is a plant saccharide that is not found in animals. It is a component of cell wall and may be useful as a natural pharmaceutical.
L-tryprophan is an essential amino acid that acts like a natural mood regulator that is necessary for normal growth in infants and for nitrogen balance in adults.
Trehalose is a non-reducing sugar. It is thought to provide plants and animals with the ability to withstanding periods of dehydration.
Guaiazulene is a bicyclic sesquiterpene that is a constituent of some essential oils, mainly oil of guaiac and chamomile oil, which also serve as its commercial sources.
Thioctic acid (DL-α-Lipoic acid) is a cyclic disulfide antioxidant that interconverts with its reduced dithiol form.
Oxaceprol is an anti-inflammatory drug used in the treatment of osteoarthritis.
R(+)-α-lipoic acid, a physiological form of thioctic acid, is a strong antioxidant that relieves diabetic neuropathic symptoms. It shows superior antioxidative effects to its racemate.
Pergolide Mesylate is an antiparkinsonian agent which functions as a dopaminergic agonist.

Cabozantinib malate (XL184) is the malate of Cabozantinib, a potent VEGFR2 inhibitor with IC50 of 0.035 nM and also inhibits c-Met, Ret, Kit, Flt-1/3/4, Tie2, and AXL with IC50 of 1.3 nM, 4 nM, 4.6 nM, 12 nM/11.3 nM/6 nM, 14.3 nM and 7 nM in cell-free assays, respectively.
Sitagliptin phosphate monohydrate is a potent inhibitor of DPP-IV with IC50 of 19 nM in Caco-2 cell extracts.

Lithocholic acid is a toxic secondary bile acid, causes intrahepatic cholestasis, has tumor-promoting activity, its toxic effect can be protected after it activates the vitamin D receptor, PXR and FXR.
Ethambutol is a bacteriostatic antimycobacterial agent, which obstructs the formation of cell wall by inhibiting arabinosyl transferases.
Pentamidine is an inhibitor of PRL Phosphatases and also inhibits synthesis of DNA, RNA and protein.
Mirabegron is a selective β3-adrenoceptor agonist with EC50 of 22.4 nM.
Acebutolol is a β-adrenergic receptors antagonist used in the treatment of hypertension, angina pectoris and cardiac arrhythmias.
Ampiroxicam is a nonselective cyclooxygenase inhibitor uesd as anti-inflammatory drug.
Desloratadine is a potent antagonist for  human histamine H1 receptor with IC50 of 51 nM.
Hyoscyamine is an AChR inhibitor with IC50 of 7.5 nM.
Ouabain is a selective Na+/K+, -ATPase inhibitor, binds to α2 /α3 subunit with Ki of 41 nM/15 nM.
Allylthiourea is a metabolic inhibitor that selective inhibits ammonia oxidation.
Sennoside B, a kind of irritant laxative isolated from rhei rhizome, inhibits PDGF-stimulated cell proliferation.
Avanafil is a highly selective PDE5 inhibitor with IC50 of 5.2 nM, >121-fold selectivity over other PDEs.
Sodium Picosulfate inhibits absorption of water and electrolytes, and increases their secretion.
Tolcapone is a selective, potent and reversible of catechol-O-methyl transferase (COMT) inhibitor with Ki of 30 nM.
Probenecid is a classical competitive inhibitor of organic anion transport, which is also a TRPV2 agonist and an inhibitor of TAS2R16.
Procaine is an inhibitor of sodium channel, NMDA receptor and nAChR with IC50 of 60 μM, 0.296 mM and 45.5 μM, which is also an inhibitor of 5-HT3 with KD of 1.7 μM.
Homatropine Methylbromide is muscarinic AChR antagonist, inhibits endothelial and smooth muscle muscarinic receptors of WKY-E and SHR-E with IC50 of 162.5 nM and 170.3 nM, respectively.
Homatropine Bromide is an muscarinic AChR antagonist, inhibits endothelial and smooth muscle muscarinic receptors of WKY-E and SHR-E with IC50 of 162.5 nM and 170.3 nM, respectively.
Hydroxyzine is a histamine H1-receptor antagonist, inhibits binding of [3H]pyrilamine/[3H]desloratadine to human histamine H1 receptor with IC50 of 10 nM/19 nM.
Aclidinium Bromide inhibits human muscarinic AChR M1, M2, M3, M4 and M5 with Ki of 0.1 nM, 0.14 nM, 0.14 nM, 0.21 nM and 0.16 nM, respectively.
Diphemanil Methylsulfate is a quaternary ammonium anticholinergic, it binds muscarinic acetycholine receptors (mAchR).
Vitamin D2 is a selective inhibitor of mammalian DNA polymerase A (pol A) with IC50 of 123 mM.
Doxapram HCl inhibits TASK-1, TASK-3, TASK-1/TASK-3 heterodimeric channel function with EC50 of 410 nM, 37 μM, 9 μM, respectively.
Dibucaine HCl is a local anesthetics.
Methazolamide is a carbonic anhydrase inhibitor with Ki of 50 nM, 14 nM and 36 nM for hCA I, hCA II and bCA IV isoforms, respectively.
Norethindrone is a synthetic progestin, which mimic the actions of the endogenous ovarian hormone progesterone.
Olsalazine Sodium is an anti-inflammatory prodrug, which consists of two 5-ASA moieties linked by an azo bond.
Nafcillin sodium reversibly inhibits β-lactamase with Kd of 33 mM.
Tetrahydrozoline HCl is an imidazoline derivative with alpha receptor agonist activity.
Toltrazuril is an antiprotozoal agent that acts upon Coccidia parasites.
Pheniramine Maleate is an antihistamine with anticholinergic properties used to treat allergic conditions such as hay fever or urticaria.
Estradiol cypionate is the 17 β-cyclopentylpropinate ester of estradiol, which inhibits ET-1 synthesis via estrogen receptor.
Bisacodyl (INN) is a stimulant laxative drug that works directly on the colon to produce a bowel movement.
Carbimazole is an imidazole antithyroid agent.
Valdecoxib is a potent and selective inhibitor of COX-2 with IC50 of 5 nM.
Valganciclovir HCl is a prodrug for ganciclovir with antiviral activity used to treat cytomegalovirus infections.
Nabumetone is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug and its active metabolite inhibits the COX.
Sertraline HCl is a 5-HT antagonist with Ki of 13 nM.
Spironolactone is a potent antagonist of the androgen receptor with IC50 of 77 nM.
Retapamulin is a topical antibiotic, which binds to both E. coli and S. aureus ribosomes with similar potencies with Kd of 3 nM.
Methyclothiazide is a substituted benzothiadiazide, used to treat high blood pressure and fluid retention caused by various conditions including heart disease.
Ropivacaine HCl is an anaesthetic agent and blocks impulse conduction in nerve fibres through inhibiting sodium ion influx reversibly.
Sodium Nitroprusside  Dihydrate is a potent vasodilator working through releasing NO spontaneously in blood.
Erythromycin Ethylsuccinate, an oral macrolide antibiotic produced by Streptomyces erythreus, reversibly binds to the 50S ribosome of bacteria, and inhibits protein synthesis.
Levobupivacaine HCl, the pure S(-)-enantiomer of bupivacaine, is a reversible neuronal sodium channel inhibitor, used as a long-acting local anesthetic.
Ronidazole is an antiprotozoal agent.
Vitamin D3 is a form of vitamin D, binds and activates a H305F/H397Y mutant vitamin D receptor (VDR) with EC50 of 300 nM.
Escitalopram Oxalate is a selective serotonin (5-HT) reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) with Ki of 0.89 nM.
Guanabenz Acetate is an selective agonist of α2a-adrenergic receptor, α2b-adrenergic receptor and α2c-adrenergic receptor with pEC50 of 8.25, 7.01 and ~5, respectively.
Tinidazole is an anti-parasitic drug.
Guanidine HCl, the crystalline compound of strong alkalinity formed by the oxidation of guanine, is a normal product of protein metabolism and a protein denaturant.
Griseofulvin, a production from some strains of the mold Penicillium griseofulvumam, is able to inhibit cell mitosis by interfering with microtubule function.
Decamethonium Bromide is a nicotinic AChR partial agonist and neuromuscular blocking agent.
Sodium nitrite is a myeloperoxidase inhibitor with IC50 of 1.3 μM.
Zinc pyrithione is an antifungal and antibacterial agent disrupting membrane transport by blocking the proton pump.
Propranolol HCl is a competitive non-selective beta-adrenergic receptors inhibitor with IC50 of 12 nM.
Mequinol (4-Methoxyphenol) is a depigmentation agent.
Mefenamic Acid is a competitive inhibitor of COX-1 and COX-2.
Ticagrelor is the first reversibly binding oral P2Y12 receptor antagonist with Ki of 2 nM.
Triamterene blocks epithelial Na+ channel (ENaC) in a voltage-dependent manner with IC50 of 4.5 μM.
Sulfacetamide Sodium is an anti-biotic.
Spiramycin is a 16-membered ring macrolide (antibiotic).
Vitamin A is a group of unsaturated nutritional hydrocarbons, that includes retinol, retinal, retinoic acid, and several provitamin A carotenoids, among which beta-carotene is the most important.
Lomerizine dihydrochloride is a relatively new L- and T-type calcium channel blocker used in the treatment of migraine.
Levobetaxolol exhibits a higher affinity at cloned human β1 and β2 receptors with Ki value of 0.76 nM and 32.6 nM, respectively.
Loxapine Succinate is a D2DR and D4DR inhibitor, serotonergic receptor antagonist and also a dibenzoxazepine anti-psychotic agent.
Flumethasone is a glucocorticoid receptor agonist, this complex binds to the nucleus causing a variety of genetic activation and repressions.
Halobetasol Propionate is an anti-inflammatory and a dermatologic agent commonly used to treat psoriasis.

Fenspiride is a bronchodilator with anti-inflammatory properties, inhibiting phosphodiesterase 4 and phosphodiesterase 3 activities with logIC50 values of 4.16 and 3.44, respectively, in human isolated bronchi.
Ifenprodil is an atypical noncompetitive antagonist at the NMDA receptor, it interacts with high affinity at a homogeneous population of NMDA receptors in neonatal rat forebrain with IC50 of 0.3 μM.
Pramoxine is a topical local anesthetic that has been shown to have antipruritic properties.
Difluprednate (difluoroprednisolone butyrate acetate, or DFBA) is a synthetic difluorinated prednisolone derivative, it is originally developed for dermatologic applications.
Droperidol is a potent antagonist of dopamine subtype 2 receptors in the limbic system.
Halcinonide is a high potency corticosteroid used in topical preparations as an anti-inflammatory agent.
Dexlansoprazole, the dextrorotatory enantiomer of lansoprazole, is a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) formulated to have dual delayed-release properties.
Esmolol is a cardioselective b-blocker, used to control rapid heartbeats or abnormal heart rhythms.
Voglibose is an N-substituted derivative of valiolamine, excellent inhibitory activity against α-glucosidases and its action against hyperglycemia and various disorders caused by hyperglycemia.
Eprosartan is a nonpeptide angiotensin II receptor antagonist, [3H]-eprosartan binds to the AT1 receptor with KD of 0.83 nM in rat vascular smooth muscle cells.
Diminazene is a di-amidine also known as 4,4-(1-Triazene–1,3–diyl)bis(benzenecarboximidamide), used as an effective trypanocidal agent.
Closantel is a gram positive antibacterial activity inhibitor, inhibiting the KinA/Spo0F system with IC50 of 3.8 μM.
Closantel is a gram positive antibacterial activity inhibitor, inhibiting the KinA/Spo0F system with IC50 of 3.8 μM.

Clofazimine is a rhimophenazine dye, originally developed for the treatment of tuberculosis, it has both antimicrobial and antiinflammatory activity, postulated mechanisms of action include intercalation of clofazimine with bacterial DNA and increasing levels of cellular phospholipase A2.
Estradiol benzoate is an estradiol analog which binds to the human, murine and chicken ERα with IC50 of 22-28 nM.
Dicloxacillin is a β-lactamase resistant penicillin similar to oxacillin and it has activity against gram-positive/negative aerobic and anaerobic bacteria.
Desvenlafaxine Succinate is a new serotonin (5-HT) transporter and norepinephrine (NE) transporter reuptake inhibitor with Ki of 40.2 nM and 558.4 nM respectively.
Desvenlafaxine is a serotonin (5-HT) and norepinephrine (NE) reuptake inhibitor with Ki of 40.2 nM and 558.4 nM, respectively.
Triclabendazole is a benzimidazole, it binds to tubulin impairing intracellular transport mechanisms and interferes with protein synthesis.
Histamine is an organic nitrogen compound, acts on target cells in mammalian brain via stimulation of Histamine 1/2.
Pefloxacin Mesylate Dihydrate is the third generation of fluoroquinolone class of antibacterials, which inhibits Topoisomerase II activity and DNA replication.
Sulconazole Nitrate is an imidazole derivative with broad-spectrum antifungal activity.
Tilmicosin is a macrolide antibiotic.
Timolol Maleate is a non-selective, beta-adrenergic receptor antagonist for β1/β2 with Ki of 1.97 nM/2.0 nM.
Tolazoline is a non-selective competitive α-adrenergic receptor antagonist.
Sodium phenylbutyrate is a histone deacetylase inhibitor, used to treat urea cycle disorders.
Troxipide is a novel systemic non-antisecretory gastric cytoprotective agent with anti-ulcer, anti-inflammatory and mucus secreting properties irrespective of pH of stomach or duodenum.

Levodropropizine possess anti-allergic and inhibits histamine receptor, reduces cough by interfering with stimulus activation of peripheral endings of sensory nerves and by modulation of neuropeptides involved in the cough reflex.
Clorprenaline HCl is a β2-receptor agonist, it has a significant expansion of the bronchial effect.
Carprofen inhibits canine COX2 with IC50 of 30 nM.
Dropropizine is a racemic non-opiate antitussive agent, it is used as a cough suppressant.
Cyclizine 2HCl is a piperazine derivative with Histamine H1 receptor antagonist activity.
Dinitolmide is a broad-spectrum anticoccidial drug.
Pentoxyverine Citrate is an antitussive (cough suppressant) commonly used for cough associated with illnesses like common cold.
Azithromycin Dihydrate is an acid stable orally administered macrolide antimicrobial drug, structurally related to erythromycin.

Ampicillin Trihydrate is a β-lactam antibiotic, which inhibits bacterial cell-wall synthesis (peptidoglycan cross-linking) by inactivating transpeptidases on the inner surface of the bacterial cell membrane.
Amfenac Sodium monohydrate is a non-steroidal analgesic anti-inflammatory drug with acetic acid moiety. The IC50 values for COX1 and COX2 is 250 nM and 150 nM, respectively.
Penfluridol is a highly potent, first generation diphenylbutylpiperidine antipsychotic.
Ethamsylate is a haemostatic drug, which inhibits biosynthesis and action of prostaglandins, and increases capillary endothelial resistance as and platelet adhesion.
Chlorzoxazone is a muscle-relaxing drug,and a probe for human liver cytochrome P-450IIE1.
Bezafibrate is the first clinically tested dual and pan-PPAR co-agonism.
Penicillin G Sodium is a β-lactam antibiotic produced by Penicillin spp.
Benzoic acid is a colorless crystalline solid and a simple aromatic carboxylic acid, used as a food preservative.
Benzethonium chloride is a potent inhibitor of nAChRs, it inhibits α4β2 nAChRs and α7 nAChRs with IC50 of 49 nM and 122 nM, respectively.
Doxycycline is a member of the tetracycline antibiotics group, and is commonly used to treat a variety of infections. It is also an inhibitor of matrix metallo-proteinases (MMP).
Doxofylline is a phosphodiesterase inhibitor and a xanthine derivative drug for asthma.
Benzydamine hydrochloride is a topical nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) with anti-inflammatory, analgesic, antipyretic and local anesthetic activity.
Chlorpropamide is a sulfonylurea class drug for type 2 diabetes mellitus.
Cyromazine is a triazine insect growth regulator used as an insecticide and an acaricide.
Teriflunomide is the active metabolite of leflunomide, inhibiting pyrimidine de novo synthesis by blocking the enzyme dihydroorotate dehydrogenase, used as an immunomodulatory agent.
Coumarin is a secondary phytochemical with hepatotoxic and carcinogenic properties.

Choline chloride is a quaternary ammonium salt used as an additive for animal feed. It is important as a precursor of acetylcholine, as a methyl donor in various metabolic processes, and in lipid metabolism.
Cetylpyridinium chloride is a cationic quaternary ammonium compound used as oropharyngeal antiseptic.
1-Hexadecanol is a fatty alcohol used to make other chemicals.
Sulfaguanidine is a sulfonamide used as an anti-infective agent.

Trometamol is a proton acceptor used to treat acidemia. It inhibits both isoforms of cyclooxygenases (COX1 and COX2), thereby blocking the conversion of arachidonic acid to pro-inflammatory pro-prostaglandins.
Uracil is one of the four nucleobases in the nucleic acid of RNA can be used for drug delivery and as a pharmaceutical.
Climbazole is a broad-spectrum imidazole antifungal agent that can provide anti-dandruff benefits.
Mezlocillin sodium is a penicillin beta-lactam antibiotic used in the treatment of bacterial infections caused by susceptible, usually gram-positive, organisms.
Nicardipine is a dihydropyridine calcium-channel blocking agent used for the treatment of vascular disorders.

Nifuroxazide is a cell-permeable and orally available nitrofuran-based antidiarrheal agent that effectively suppresses the activation of cellular STAT1/3/5 transcription activity with IC50 of 3 μM against IL-6-induced STAT3 activation in U3A cells.
Penciclovir is a purine acyclic nucleoside analogue with potent antiviral activity.
Tiratricol (also known as TRIAC or triiodothyroacetic acid) is a thyroid hormone analogue.
Domiphen bromide is a quaternary ammonium antiseptic with actions as a cationic surfactant.
Salicylanilides are a group of compounds with a wide range of biological activities including antiviral potency, antibacterial (including antimycobacterial) and antifungal activities.
Sasapyrine (salsalate) is a nonsteroidal oral anti-inflammatory agent.
Cyclandelate is an effective inhibitor of rat hepatic acycloenzyme A: cholesterol acyltransferase (ACAT) with IC50 of 80 μM.
Betamipron is a chemical compound which is used together with panipenem to inhibit panipenem uptake into the renal tubule and prevent nephrotoxicity.
Chlorquinaldol is an antimicrobial agent used for local antisepsy.
Broxyquinoline is an antiprotozoal agent and able to release oxygen free radicals from the water in mucous membranes.
Ethacridine lactate monohydrate is an aromatic organic compound based on acridine used as an antiseptic agent.
Bemegride is a central nervous system stimulant and antidote for barbiturate poisoning.
Tolperisone HCl is an ion channel blocker and centrally-acting muscle relaxant.
Florfenicol is a fluorinated synthetic analog of thiamphenicol with broad-spectrum, primarily bacteriostatic activity.
Verapamil HCl is an L-type calcium channel blocker that is a class IV anti-arrhythmia agent.
Furaltadone HCl is an antibacterial and has distinct curative effect in the treatment of coccidiosis.
Isosorbide is a heterocyclic compound that is derived from glucose, used as a diuretic.
Cysteamine is an agent for the treatment of nephropathic cystinosis and an antioxidant.
Clofibric acid is a PPARα agonist and hypolipidemic agent.
Chromocarb is a vasoprotectant.
Chlorocresol is an activator of ryanodine receptor.
Benzocaine is the ethyl ester of p-aminobenzoic acid (PABA), it is a local anesthetic commonly used as a topical pain reliever or in cough drops.
Montelukast selectively antagonizes leukotriene D 4 (LTD4) by binding to it so that block the action of leukotriene D4 on the cysteinyl leukotriene receptor CysLT1.
Dirithromycin is a macrolide glycopeptide antibiotic by binding to the 50S subunit of the 70S bacterial ribosome to inhibit the translocation of peptides.
Sucralose is an artificial and noncaloric sweetener, not broken down by the body.
Valnemulin HCl is a broad-spectrum bacteriostatic agent inhibiting protein synthesis in bacteria by binding to the peptidyl transferase component of the 50S subunit of ribosomes.
Liothyronine Sodium is the most potent form of thyroid hormone acting on the body to increase the basal metabolic rate, affect protein synthesis.

Azaperone crosses the blood-brain barrier and binds to both DI and D2 receptors, being an antagonist of Dopamine receptors with sedative and antiemetic effects, which is used mainly as a tranquilizer in veterinary medicine.
Benzbromarone is a CYP2C9 inhibitor, it binds to CYP2C9 with Ki value of 19.3 nM.
Piperacillin is a semisynthetic, broad-spectrum, ampicillin derived ureidopenicillin antibiotic proposed for pseudomonas infections.
Mevastatin is a competitive inhibitor of HMG-Coenzyme A (HMG-CoA) reductase with a binding affinity 10,000 times greater than the HMG-CoA substrate itself.
Erythritol is a sugar alcohol used as low-calorie sweetener.
Mexiletine HCl belongs to Class IB anti-arrhythmic group of medicines, inhibits sodium channels to reduce the inward sodium current.
Fidaxomicin is a narrow spectrum macrocyclic antibiotic that inhibits RNA polymerase sigma subunit.
Fluorometholone Acetate is a synthetic corticosteroid, used in the treatment of steroid responsive inflammatory conditions of the eye.

Oxybuprocaine HCl is a local anesthetic, which is used especially in ophthalmology and otolaryngology.  Oxybuprocaine binds to sodium channel and reversibly stabilizes the neuronal membrane which decreases its permeability to sodium ions

Oxaprozin is a non-narcotic, non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to relieve the inflammation, swelling, stiffness, and joint pain associated with osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.
Pilocarpine HCl is a nonselective muscarinic acetylcholine receptor agonist used to produce an experimental model of epilepsy.
Phenazopyridine HCl is a local analgesic that has been used in urinary tract disorders. It is an inhibitor of sodium channel protein type 1 subunit alpha.
Primaquine Diphosphate is a transmission-blocking anti-malarial clinically available, displaying a marked activity against gametocytes of all species of human malaria.

Cepharanthine is a biscoclaurine alkaloid inhibiting tumor necrosis factor (TNF)-α-mediated NFκB stimulation, plasma membrane lipid peroxidation and platelet aggregation and suppressing cytokine production.
Bergapten is a psoralen that can be photoactivated and is capable of crossing-linking DNA, covalently modifying proteins and lipids, and consequently inhibiting cell replication.
Doxylamine succinate competitively inhibits histamine at H1 receptors with substantial sedative and anticholinergic effects.
Cetrimonium Bromide is a known component of the broad-spectrum antiseptic cetrimide, which is a mixture of different quaternary ammonium salts.
Deoxycorticosterone acetate is a steroid hormone used for intramuscular injection for replacement therapy of the adrenocortical steroid.
Serotonin HCl is a monoamine neurotransmitter and Endogenous 5-HT receptor agonist.
Tranylcypromine is a monoamine oxidase inhibitor, which inhibits CYP2A6 with Ki of 0.08 μM and 0.2 μM in cDNA-expressing microsomes and Human Liver Microsomes, respectively.
Prucalopride is a selective, high affinity 5-HT4 receptor agonist. The Ki values of prucalopride for human 5-HT(4a) and 5-HT(4b) receptor are 2.5 nM and 8 nM, respectively.
Bromfenac Sodium is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID), which has anti-inflammatory activity and may block prostaglandin synthesis by inhibiting cyclooxygenase 1 and 2.
Flopropione is a spasmolytic or antispasmodic agent, and acts as a 5-HT1A receptor antagonist.
Sulfamethoxypyridazine is a long-acting sulfonamide for treatment of Dermatitis herpetiformis.
Mechlorethamine is the prototype of alkylating agents, it works by binding to DNA, crosslinking two strands and preventing cell duplication.
Epinastine is an antihistamine and mast cell stabilizer that is used in eye drops to treat allergic conjunctivitis.
Quinacrine 2HCl is a lipophilic cationic drug with multiple actions that is commonly used as an anti-protozoal agent. Quinacrine is an effective  phospholipase A2 inhibitor.
Buspirone is a serotonergic (5HT(1A) receptor agonist) anxiolytic drug with some D(2) dopaminergic effect, used for anxiety disorders.
Alizapride HCl is a dopamine receptor antagonist used for the theropy of nausea and vomiting.
Luliconazole is a broad-spectrum antifungal drug.
Vilazodone HCl is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) and  a partial agonist of 5-HT1A receptors, used for the treatment of major depressive disorder.
Tamibarotene is a synthetic retinoic acid receptor (RAR) agonist with high specificity for RARα and RARβ over RARγ.
Efaproxiral Sodium is a synthetic allosteric modifier of hemoglobin, used for brain metastases originating from breast cancer.
Etofibrate is a combination of clofibrate and niacin, used to treat hyperlipemia.
Nicaraven is a hydroxyl radical scavenger with antivasospastic and neuroprotective effects.
Brimonidine Tartrate is a highly selective α-adrenergic receptor agonist with EC50 of 0.45 nM for the α2A adrenoreceptor, and used to treat open-angle glaucoma or ocular hypertension.
Diacerein is an inhibitor of pro-inflammatory cytokine Interleukin-1B (IL-1B) production, prescribed for osteoarthritis and chronic inflammatory arthritis.
Flufenamic Acid is an anti-inflammatory agent, and also acts as an ion channel modulator.
Vinorelbine Tartrate is a semi-synthetic vinca alkaloid, and inhibits mitosis through interaction with tubulin.
Oxiracetam, a cyclic derivative of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), is used as a nootropic drug to improve memory and learning.
Rotigotine is a dopamine receptor agonist, used in the treatment of Parkinson's disease and restless legs syndrome.
Bambuterol HCl is a potent β-adrenoceptor agonist, used in the treatment of asthma.
Carteolol HCl is a β-adrenoceptor antagonist, used for the treatment of glaucoma.
Demeclocycline HCl is a tetracycline antibiotic via inhibition of protein synthesis by bacteria, used for the treatment of bacterial infections.
Meclofenoxate (Centrophenoxine) HCl is an anti-aging drug used to treat the symptoms of senile dementia and Alzheimer's disease, and also inhibits the activity of cholinephosphotransferase.
Tasimelteon is a melatonin MT1 and MT2 receptor agonist.
Nelfinavir Mesylate is a potent HIV protease inhibitor with Ki of 2 nM.
Cyclobenzaprine HCl is a muscle relaxant by blocking pain sensations, used for the treatment of muscle spasms A 5-HT2 receptor antagonist.
Chloroprocaine HCl is a local anesthetic during surgical procedures.
Ospemifene is a non-hormonal selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM), used for the treatment of dyspareunia.
Anidulafungin (LY303366), an echinocandin derivative, inhibits glucan synthase activity, used as an antifungal drug.
Chlorambucil is a nitrogen mustard alkylating agent, used in the treatment of chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
Metoclopramide HCl is a selective dopamine D2 receptor antagonist, used for the treatment of nausea and vomiting.
Digoxin is a classical Na,K-ATPase inhibitor, with selectivity for the α2β3 isoform over the common α1β1 isoform, used in the treatment of atrial fibrillation and heart failure.
Labetalol HCl is a dual antagonist for both selective alpha1-adrenergic and nonselective beta-adrenergic receptors, used in the treatment of high blood pressure.
Diphenidol HCl is a potent antagonist of muscarinic M2 and M3 receptor with pKb of 6.72 and 7.02, respectively, used in the treatment of vomiting and vertigo.
Promethazine HCl is a potent histamine H1 receptor antagonist, used as a sedative and antiallergic medication.
Procainamide HCl is a sodium channel blocker, and also a DNA methyltransferase inhibitor, used in the treatment of cardiac arrhythmias.
Meclofenamate Sodium is a dual COX-1/COX-2 inhibitor with IC50 of 40 nM and 50 nM, respectively, used in the treatment of joint, muscular pain, arthritis and dysmenorrhea.

Salmeterol Xinafoate is a long-acting β2-adrenergic receptor agonist with anti-inflammatory effects, used in the treatment of asthma symptoms and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) symptoms.
Mupirocin is an isoleucyl t-RNA synthetase inhibitor, used in the treatment of bacterial skin infections.
Dicoumarol is a competitive NADPH quinone oxidoreductase (NQO1) inhibitor,and used as an anticoagulant by interfering with the metabolism of vitamin K.
(R)-(+)-Atenolol binds to but does not activate beta-adrenergic receptors thereby blocking the actions of endogenous or exogenous beta-adrenergic agonists.
Anisotropine Methylbromide is a quaternary ammonium compound, used as treatment adjunct in peptic ulcer.
Anisindione is a synthetic anticoagulant and an indanedione derivative.
Anisotropine Methylbromide is a quaternary ammonium compound, used as treatment adjunct in peptic ulcer.
Benzthiazide is a thiazide diuretic, promoting water loss from the body.

Bromocriptine, an ergoline derivative, is a dopamine agonist that is used in the treatment of pituitary tumors, Parkinson's disease (PD), hyperprolactinaemia, neuroleptic malignant syndrome, and type 2 diabetes.
Carbadox is an anti-inflammatory drug used in the treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
Clorgiline is an irreversible and selective inhibitor of monoamine oxidase A (MAO-A).
Disopyramide (DISO) is a widely used Class 1a antiarrhythmic agent that is employed in the treatment of ventricular and supraventricular arrhythmias.
Ethoxzolamide is an inhibitor of the metalloenzyme carbonic anhydrase, which inhibits hCA I, hCA II, hCA IV, hCA VI and hCA VII with Ki of 25 nM, 8 nM, 93 nM, 43 nM and 0.8 nM, respectively.
Isoetharine is a selective beta2-adrenergic receptor agonist, which gives quick relief for bronchospasm and asthma.
Meclocycline is a tetracycline antibiotic. Meclocycline sulfosalicylate prevents bacterial protein synthesis through 30S ribosome-mediated chain elongation and blocks amino-acyl tRNA A-site binding.
Methoxamine is an α1-adrenergic receptor agonist, it induces vasoconstriction of skin and splanchnic blood vessels.
Oxethazaine is a strong local anaesthetic, which acts in a number of ways to numb the walls of the stomach and relieve the pain associated with hyperacidic conditions.
Oxprenolol is a non-selective beta blocker with some intrinsic sympathomimetic activity.

Pentoxifylline is a competitive nonselective phosphodiesterase inhibitor which raises intracellular cAMP, activates PKA, inhibits TNF and leukotriene synthesis, and reduces inflammation and innate immunity.
Piromidic acid is a quinolone antibiotic.
Procyclidine HCl blocks the effects of certain chemicals in the brain, and thus is used to treat Parkinson's disease or extrapyramidal side-effects caused by other medicines.
Ractopamine HCl (RAC), a beta-agonist with established nutrient partitioning capabilities, promotes skeletal muscle accretion at the expense of fat deposition.
Terfenadine is an antihistamine, generally completely metabolizes to the active form fexofenadine in the liver by the enzyme cytochrome P450 CYP3A4 isoform.
Thiostrepton is a natural cyclic oligopeptide antibiotic, derived from several strains of strepromycetes.
Tacrine is a centrally acting anticholinesterase and indirect cholinergic agonist.
Pimozide is an antipsychotic drug of the diphenylbutylpiperidine class, which is a strong inhibitor of CYP2D6.
Carbachol is a cholinomimetic drug that binds and activates the acetylcholine receptor.
Cinoxacin is a chemotherapeutic agent that inhibits bacterial DNA synthesis.
Glafenine is an anthranilic acid derivative with analgesic properties, which has been used previously in the treatment of pain.
Phthalylsulfacetamide is a sulfonamide with a high solubility and suited for systemic use for diseases of the eye.
Pinacidil is known to augment a time-independent outward current in cardiac tissues by activating the ATP-regulated potassium channels.
Suxibuzone is a prodrug of the non steroidal anti inflammatory drug phenylbutazone, and is commonly used in horses.

Carbenoxolone, a synthetic derivative of glycyrrhetinic acid, is also used as a blocker of the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase (11β-HSD), of pannexon membrane channels (comprising 6 subunits of pannexin) and the related innexon channels.
Dicyclomine is an anticholinergic drug, a chemical released from nerves that stimulates muscles, by blocking the receptors for acetylcholine on smooth muscle (a type of muscle).
Mepenzolate is a post-ganglionic parasympathetic inhibitor, it decreases gastric acid and pepsin secretion and suppresses spontaneous contractions of the colon.
Aceclidine is a parasympathomimetic miotic agent used to decreases intraocular pressure.
Imipramine is a tricyclic antidepressant (TCA) of the dibenzazepine group, mainly used in the treatment of major depression and enuresis (inability to control urination).
Proadifen is an inhibitor of cytochrome P450 enzymes, preventing some types of drug metabolism.

Pyrilamine is a histamine H1 receptor inverse agonist, it binds to a G protein-coupled form of the receptor and promotes a G protein-coupled inactive state of the H1 receptor that interferes with the Gq/11-mediated signaling.
Fosfomycin tromethamine is a phosphonic acid bactericidal agent with in vitro activity against most urinary tract pathogens.
Mefloquine HCl is a blood schizonticide by inhibiting hemozoin formation, used as an antimalarial drug.

Eltrombopag, a member of the biarylhydrazone class, is a nonpeptide agonist of the thrombopoietin receptor (TpoR), used to treat chronic hepatitis C-associated thrombocytopenia and chronic immune (idiopathic) thrombocytopenia (ITP).

6-Mercaptopurine Monohydrate is a widely used antileukemic agent and immunosuppressive drug that inhibits de novo purine synthesis through incorporation of thiopurine methyltransferase metabolites into DNA and RNA.
Vinblastine sulfate inhibits microtubule formation and suppresses nAChR activity with IC50 of 8.9 μM in a cell-free assay, used to treat certain kinds of cancer.
Acetazolamide, a potent carbonic anhydrase (CA) inhibitor, is commonly used in clinical practice as an immediate and readily available option for acute reduction of intraocular pressure(IOP).

17-Hydroxyprogesterone?(17-OHP) is an?endogenous?progestogen?as well as?chemical intermediate?in the?biosynthesis?of other?steroid hormones, including the?corticosteroids?and the?androgens?and the estrogens.

4-Aminoantipyrine(Ampyrone) is a metabolite of aminopyrine with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. It is used as a reagent for biochemical reactions producing peroxides or phenols.
Synonyms: Ampyrone, NSC 60242, Metapirazone, Solnapyrin-A, Solvapyrin-A,  Minoazophene, Aminoazophene,  Aminoantipyrin

Information: 4-Aminobenzoic acid is an intermediate in the synthesis of tetrahydrofolic acid in many non-mammalian organisms, including bacteria and fungi.
Aceglutamide is a psychostimulant, nootropic agent which functions as a prodrug to glutamine with improved potency and stability.
Acetylleucine is a drug used in the treatment of vertigo.

Ademetionine Disulfate Tosylate is the disulfate-tosylate mixed salt of a mixture of diastereoisomers of the ademetionine ions. Ademetionine possesses anti-inflammatory activity and has been used in treatment of chronic liver disease.
Camphor is a waxy, flammable, white or transparent solid with a strong aroma, often used to relieve pain, reduce itching and treat fungal infections. It can activate TRPV1  and TRPV3.
Cefotaxime sodium salt is a third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic broad-spectrum antibiotic with activity against numerous Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.
Chloroxylenol is a broad-spectrum antimicrobial chemical compound used to control bacteria, algae, fungi and virus.
2-Aminoheptane is a nasal decongestant drug which is a sympathomimetic stimulant and vasoconstrictor.

Ethylparaben is the ethyl ester of p-hydroxybenzoic acid, used as an antifungal preservative and food additive. It is a standardized chemical allergen. The physiologic effect of ethylparaben is by means of Increased Histamine Release, and Cell-mediated Immunity.
Fenbufen is an orally active phenylalkanoic derivative with anti-inflammatory, analgesic and antipyretic activity. It is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug.
Fenofibric acid is a fibrate that acts as a lipid-lowering agent, decreasing low-density lipoprotein cholesterol and triglycerides.
Furazolidone is a synthetic nitrofuran derivative with bactericidal or bacteriostatic activity when used against Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.
Iopamidol is a non-ionic, water-soluble radiographic contrast agent.
Methylene Blue is used as a dye in chromoendoscopy. It Inhibits tau filament formation with IC50 of 1.9μM. Also it inhibits soluble guanylyl cyclase.
Synonyms: Basic Blue 9, Tetramethylthionine chloride, methylthioninium chloride, CI-52015

Information: Nitrofurantoin is an antibiotic used to treat bladder infections.It inhibits bacterial DNA, RNA, and cell wall protein synthesis.

Pantoprazole is a proton pump inhibitor drug that inhibits gastric acid secretion. It works on gastric parietal cells to irreversibly inhibit (H+/K+)-ATPase function and suppress the production of gastric acid.
Salicylic acid is a beta hydroxy acid that occurs as a natural compound in plants which is an inhibitor of ethylene biosynthesis and cyclooxygenase activity.
Triclosan is a diphenyl ether derivative used in cosmetics and toilet soaps as an antiseptic. It has some bacteriostatic and fungistatic action.
Trihexyphenidyl hydrochloride is an antiparkinsonian agent of the antimuscarinic class.

Trimetazidine dihydrochloride is a kind of drug for treatment of chronic ischemic disorders. It improves myocardial glucose utilization through inhibition of fatty acid metabolism, known as fatty acid oxidation inhibitor.
Urethane is a kind of antineoplastic agent that is also used as a veterinary anesthetic. It is a intermediate in organic synthesis.
Synonyms: Carbamic acid ethyl ester, Ethyl carbamate, Ethylurethane

Information: Xylitol is a sugar alcohol derived from XYLOSE by reduction of the carbonyl group. It is as sweet as sucrose and used as a noncariogenic sweetener.

8-Hydroxyquinoline is an antiseptic with mild fungistatic, bacteriostatic, anthelmintic, and amebicidal action. It is also used as a reagent and metal chelator, as a carrier for radio-indium for diagnostic purposes.
Aminoguanidine is a diamine oxidase and nitric oxide synthase inhibitor. It acts to reduce levels of advanced glycation end products (AGEs) through interacting with 3-deoxyglucosone.
Synonyms: Pimagedine, Guanyl hydrazine, Hydrazinecarboximidamide, Imino semicarbazide, Monoaminoguanidine

Information: Azelaic acid is an organic compound produced by the ozonolysis of oleic acid and possesses antibacterial, keratolytic, comedolytic, and anti-oxidant activity.
Synonyms: Nonanedioic acid, Finacea, Azelex, Anchoic acid

Information: Bithionol is a potent inhibitor of soluble adenylyl cyclase(sAC) with an IC50 of 4 μM and has antibacterial and anthelmintic properties along with algaecide activity.
Bronopol(2-bromo-2-nitropropane-1,3-diol) is antibacterial agent, used as preservative in cosmetic industry.
Carzenide is an organic synthesis intermediate, used for synthetic drug.
Citiolone is a derivative of the amino acid cysteine, used in liver therapy.
Cloxiquine is an antibacterial, antifungal, antiaging and antituberculosis drug.

Danthron is an organic substance. It used to be a laxatica and now is currently used as an antioxidant in synthetic lubricants, in the synthesis of experimental antitumor agents, as a fungicide and as an intermediate for making dyes.
Dehydrocholic acid is a semisynthetic bile acid made from cholic acid. It is used as a cholagogue, hydrocholeretic, diuretic, and as a diagnostic aid.
Diatrizoic Acid is an organic, iodinated radiocontrast agent used in diagnostic radiography.
Diethylcarbamazine citrate is an inhibitor of arachidonic acid metabolism in filarial microfilaria and is highly specific for several parasites and does not contain any toxic metallic elements.
Diiodohydroxyquinoline is a topical therapeutic agent,a quinoline derivative, with satisfactory antibacterial properties.
DL-panthenol is a racemic mixture of D and L form of panthenol,a provitamin of B5.
Fluphenazine dihydrochloride is a phenothiazine-class D1DR and D2DR inhibitor. It also inhibits  Histamine H1 Receptor.
Halothane is a general inhalation anesthetic used for induction and maintenance of general anesthesia.

Hexylresorcinol is an organic compound with local anaesthetic, antiseptic and anthelmintic properties, is a potent inhibitor of mushroom tyrosinase. The IC50 values of hexylresorcinol for monophenolase is 1.24 μM and for diphenolase is 0.85 μM.
Piperazine, GABA receptor agonist, is an anthelmintic used in the treatment of the intestinal nematodes Ascaris lumbricoides and Enterobius vermicularis.

Sulfabenzamide is an antibacterial/antimicrobial which also exhibit their antitumor effects through multiple mechanisms including inhibition of membrane bound carbonic anhydrases, prevention of microtubule assembly, cell cycle arrest, and inhibition of angiogenesis.
Terpin hydrate is an expectorant, commonly used to loosen mucus in patients presenting with acute or chronic bronchitis, and related conditions.

Tyloxapol is a nonionic liquid polymer of the alkyl aryl polyether alcohol type, used as a surfactant to aid liquefaction, removal of mucopurulent and bronchopulmonary secretions. It also blocks plasma lipolytic activity, and thus the breakdown of triglyceride-rich lipoproteins.
Resorcinol is a chemical compound that serves as an antiseptic,an disinfectant, and a chemical intermediate for the production of many other pharmaceuticals.

Hydroquinone is a Melanin Synthesis Inhibitor. The physiologic effect of hydroquinone is by means of Depigmenting Activity. It also posesses antioxidant properties that can cause toxicity in several organs, notably the kidney.
Triacetin is a triglyceride that is used as an antifungal agent.
Synonyms: Glycerol triacetate, Glyceryl triacetate, Glycerin triacetate, 1,2,3-Triacetoxypropane

Information: Butamben is a long-duration local anesthetic used for the treatment of chronic pain.
Butylparaben is a chemical compound commonly used as an antifungal preservative in cosmetic products.
Succinylsulfathiazole, a sulfonamide, is an antibiotic.
Docusate Sodium is a laxative used to treat constipation, for constipation due to the use of opiates it maybe used with a stimulant laxative, can be taken by mouth or rectally.
Synonyms: Aerosol OT, Bis(2-ethylhexyl) sulfosuccinate sodium salt, Constonate

Information: Amodiaquine is a potent, non-competitive inhibitor of histamine N-methyl transferase with estimated Ki of 18.6 nM. It is also used as an antimalarial and anti-inflammatory agent.
Dithranol is one of the most important local therapeutic agents against the common skin disease, psoriasis.

Nitroxoline is a urinary antibacterial agent active against susceptible gram-positive and gram-negative organisms commonly found in urinary tract infections. It is a fluorquinolone that is active against bacterial gyrases.
Chlormadinone acetate is a steroidal progestin with additional antiandrogen and antigonadotropic effects, used often in combinations as an oral contraceptive. Its half-life is about 34-38 hours.

Cephalothin is a semisynthetic, beta-lactam, first-generation cephalosporin antibiotic with bactericidal activity. Cephalothin binds to and inactivates penicillin-binding proteins (PBP) located on the inner membrane of the bacterial cell wall.

Cefazolin Sodium is a semisynthetic cephalosporin analog with broad-spectrum antibiotic action due to inhibition of bacterial cell wall synthesis. It attains high serum levels and is excreted quickly via the urine.
Cefixime is an antibiotic useful for the treatment of a number of bacterial infections. It is a third generation cephalosporin.
Synonyms: Cefixima, Cefiximum, Cephoral, Cefspan, FR-17027, FK-027, CL-284635

Information: Lercanidipine is a calcium channel blocker of the dihydropyridine class.
Benzyl benzoate is one of the older preparations used to treat scabies. Scabies is a skin infection caused by the mite sarcoptes scabiei.
Synonyms: Ascabiol, Novoscabin, Scabitox, Benzoic acid benzyl ester

Information: Benzyl alcohol is used as a local anesthetic. Also, it is used in the manufacture of other benzyl compounds, as a pharmaceutic aid, and in perfumery and flavoring.
Clioquinol is an orally bioavailable, lipophilic, copper-binding, halogenated 8-hydroxyquinoline with antifungal, antiparasitic and potential antitumor activities.
Acetohydroxamic acid is an Urease Inhibitor. In the urine, it acts as an antagonist of the bacterial enzyme urease.
Synonyms: N-Hydroxyacetamide, Methylhydroxamic acid, Acetic acid|oxime, Lithostat

Information: Gallic acid, known as powerful antioxidants, is an organic acid found in a variety of foods and herbs.

Levofloxacin is a broad-spectrum, third-generation fluoroquinolone antibiotic and optically active L-isomer of ofloxacin with antibacterial activity. It acts by inhibiting DNA gyrase (bacterial topoisomerase II).
Synonyms: Levofloxacin hemihydrate, Levaquin hydrate, Tavanic hydrate, Quixin hydrate, Iquix hydrate, Cravit hydrate

Information: Diflunisal is a difluorophenyl derivate of salicylic acid and a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) with antipyretic, analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The mechanism of action of diflunisal is as a Cyclooxygenase Inhibitor.

Mebendazole is a synthetic benzimidazole derivate and anthelmintic agent. Mebendazole interferes with the reproduction and survival of helminths by inhibiting the formation of their cytoplasmic microtubules, thereby selectively and irreversibly blocking glucose uptake.
Dapsone, also known as diaminodiphenyl sulfone (DDS), is an antibiotic commonly used in combination with rifampicin and clofazimine for the treatment of leprosy.
Synonyms: Dapsone, 4,4′-Diaminodiphenyl sulfone, 4,4′-Sulfonyldianiline, 4-Aminophenyl sulfone, Bis(4-aminophenyl) sulfone, DDS

Information: Dextromethorphan is an antitussive (cough suppressant) drug of the morphinan class with sedative, dissociative, and stimulant properties (at higher doses).
Fenoldopam is a selective dopamine-1 receptor (DA1) agonist with natriuretic/diuretic properties. It lowers blood pressure through arteriolar vasodilation.
Itopride hydrochloride is an AChE inhibitor. The IC50 of itopride with AChE is 2.04 +/- 0.27 μM.
Cefuroxime Sodium is a cephalosporin effective against gram-positive and gram-negative bacteria with resistance to β-lactamase.
Methylbenactyzine Bromide is a kind of muscarinic cholinergic receptor antagonist with antispasmodic activity.

Alcaftadine is a high affinity ligand for the H1 receptor, with a pKi (8.5) that is comparable with that of other H1 antihistamines. Alcaftadine is also an antagonist for H2 and H4 receptors with no affinity for the H3 receptor.
